title: Blueberry hand pies
subtitle: Individual pastry pies with a warm blueberry filling
excerpt: Individual hand-held pies made from buttery shortcrust pastry filled with a jammy blueberry compote. They bake in 20 minutes and need no utensils to eat.
sourceNotes: Hand pies (individual pastry turnovers baked rather than fried) are documented in American baking from the mid-twentieth century, particularly in the Southern US fried pie tradition. The baked version is more associated with contemporary artisan bakeries. This recipe synthesises the shortcrust pastry ratio from Joy of Cooking (Rombauer, Becker and Becker, 2006) with the blueberry compote filling method from Dorie Greenspan's Baking from My Home to Yours (2006).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked assembled hand pies on a tray, then bag. Bake from frozen at 200°C fan, adding 5 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Scale freely; the pastry and filling both double easily.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Baked hand pies keep at room temperature for 1 day. Reheat in a 160°C oven for 8 minutes.

baking.decoratingTechnique: egg wash brushed over, sprinkled with demerara sugar

content.text: The filling must be completely cool before it goes into the pastry. Hot filling makes the butter in the pastry melt on contact, producing greasy, difficult pastry that tears when sealed. Make the filling first, spread it on a plate to cool quickly, then make the pastry.

Seal the edges firmly with a fork. Press all the way around the edge with the tines of a fork, then fold the edge over and press again. A poorly sealed hand pie will burst in the oven and lose the filling. Crimp twice.

Combine the {{blueberries}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{cornflour}}, {{lemon-juice}}, and {{lemon}} zest in a small saucepan. Cook over a medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring, until the berries have burst and the mixture has thickened to a jammy consistency. Spread on a plate and refrigerate until completely cold.
Make the pastry, chill, and assemble

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}}, {{caster-sugar}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until it resembles coarse crumbs. Add 4 to 6 tablespoons of ice-cold water until the dough comes together. Refrigerate for 1 hour. Roll to about 3 mm thick and cut into 16 circles of about 12 cm diameter. Place a heaped tablespoon of the cold blueberry filling on one half of each circle, leaving a 1.5 cm border. Fold the plain half over the filling, press the edges firmly together, and seal with a fork. Place on lined baking trays.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Brush the assembled hand pies with the {{eggs}} wash and scatter {{demerara-sugar}} over. Cut a small slit in the top of each pie. Bake for 18 to 22 minutes until deep golden and the filling is just beginning to bubble through the vents. Cool on a rack for 15 minutes before eating; the filling will be hot.
